title: Team Fortress 2

notes:
64-bit doesn't launch. When starting it with `./tf_linux64`, it throws `error while loading shared libraries: libmimalloc.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory`. After installing `mimalloc-devel`, it gets to `AppFramework : Unable to load module engine.so!`, which is the expected behavior; it's what it should do given it's not running in Steam Linux Runtime 3.0. However, running it in Steam still causes it to fail to launch and nothing is displayed.

(tested on Fedora)

title: Team Fortress 2

notes:
I opened the game, clicking "Find Game", choosing "Casual" and selecting all maps/game modes (to give the best possible chance of finding a game) I click "Search." After about 30 seconds, the search ends without finding any games at all.

I used the community browser to find a game, however despite every server in the browser stating that their ping is <50ms, when joining I discover that every one that I joined has a ping of >500ms, which is not playable.

Looking online I have discovered that this is a common issue impacting a large number of users. The "solutions" that have been given (typing tf_party_leave into the console and/or verifying the game files) have not been successful. I have also tried launching the game in Proton 9.0 and still get the same issue.
title: Team Fortress 2
